description:
You are a database administrator who has just exported a set of newly-optimised SQL
  queries.  Before archiving the export, you must prove that the file on disk has not
  been corrupted in transit by verifying its SHA-256 checksum and logging the outcome.

  1. File to verify  
     /home/user/db_exports/optimized_queries.sql

  2. Known-good SHA-256 value (provided by the build system)  
     e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

  3. Required output  
     • Create a text file called  
       /home/user/db_exports/verification.log  
       (If verification.log already exists, overwrite it completely.)  
     • The file must contain exactly four lines, no extra whitespace,
       blank lines or trailing spaces:  

         filename=/home/user/db_exports/optimized_queries.sql  
         expected=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  
         actual=<the SHA-256 you calculated>  
         result=<OK or FAIL>  

       – <the SHA-256 you calculated> must be the hexadecimal digest generated
         from /home/user/db_exports/optimized_queries.sql.  
       – result must be the literal string “OK” when actual==expected,
         otherwise “FAIL”.  

  4. Constraints  
     • Accomplish the whole task from the command line in as few steps as you wish;
       a single checksum command piped or redirected is enough.  
     • Do not edit or rename the SQL file.  
     • Make sure /home/user/db_exports/verification.log is world-readable
       (chmod 644 or default).

  5. How the system will be graded  
     Automated tests will read /home/user/db_exports/verification.log and check:  
       – Line 1 is exactly “filename=/home/user/db_exports/optimized_queries.sql”  
       – Line 2 is exactly the expected value above.  
       – Line 3 matches the real checksum of the SQL file.  
       – Line 4 is “result=OK” and is consistent with the comparison of
         lines 2 and 3.

  Your job: generate the log file in the exact format described above by performing
  a SHA-256 checksum verification.
truth:
Initial file tree (already present before the agent starts):

  /home/user/db_exports/
      optimized_queries.sql      (empty file, 0 bytes)

  Contents of optimized_queries.sql:
      – the file is truly empty (no newline, no characters).

  SHA-256 of an empty file:
      e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

  After successful completion the agent must have created:

  /home/user/db_exports/verification.log  (mode 0644, user-writable)

  Expected exact contents of verification.log:

      filename=/home/user/db_exports/optimized_queries.sql
      expected=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
      actual=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
      result=OK

  Any deviation (extra spaces, additional lines, mismatched checksum, etc.)
  should cause the automated check to fail.
